text: The surgeon passes a surgical stapler through the bottom end of the rectal stump, which appears rounded. The stapler is linked to an anvil that has been inserted into the surgical field.

The surgeon uses a circular stapler to join the proximal colon to the rectal stump. As the stapler head and anvil come together, the colon and rectum are apposed, allowing the circular knife to be fired, completing the anastomosis. The stapler head is then flattened and pulled out, finalizing the laparoscopic procedure.
